title: caucalis infesta var. neglecta
intro: Caucalis infesta var. neglecta, also known as Wild Carrot or Stinking Carrot, is a plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Caucalis infesta var. neglecta include its ability to treat digestive issues, relieve menstrual cramps, and reduce fever, making it a valuable herb in folk medicine. Caucalis infesta var. neglecta can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its roots and leaves can be used to make herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
